faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Edison homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic matter that accumulates in drains, pipes, and sewage systems. In Edison, they're particularly common in bathrooms, kitchens, and basement drains where moisture and debris create ideal breeding conditions. Poor ventilation and infrequent drain cleaning contribute to infestations.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Edison?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Edison take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of affected drains. Our technicians will inspect all potential breeding sites, treat drains with specialized products, and provide preventative recommendations. You'll typically see results within 2-3 days.

faqs.question: Why does the price range from $350 to $550?
faqs.answer: The cost varies based on the size of your property, the number of drains requiring treatment, and the severity of the infestation. A single-bathroom apartment will be at the lower end, while larger homes or commercial properties with multiple bathrooms and kitchen areas may require more extensive treatment at the higher price point.

faqs.question: Are drain fly treatments safe for my Edison home or business?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for homes, businesses, and food-service establishments when applied properly. Our treatments target drain fly larvae and breeding sites without posing risks to residents, employees, or pets. We follow all New Jersey safety regulations and can work around your schedule to minimize disruption.
